stayed here for final four, $92k/key with 3 yrs IO and 1.99x DSCR.  Good location downtown but is still a 50 yr old Crowne Plaza.  Bought in 2007 for $19.5MM and started a $14MM renov from a Hampton.  $3MM held in PIP reserve because we face 2019expiration, $38MM cost basis.   were right at 101% RevPAR penetration but thats on an 83 ADR and 123 occupancy.  full recourse for loss of flag if not replaced by acceptable franchsor.  $4.4MM cash out.   PIP not sized, just est based on HVS who put them at $9,100-$16,600 per key, the $3mm is $10k/key.  WFB has pre-approved Doubletree, Embassy Suites, Hilton, Renaissance, Marriott, CY, Sheraton, aLoft, Westin, W and Intercont.    four new assets - Statler Hotel, Curio, AC by Marriott and a CY Marriott will all come to downtown.  ranked 4 of 7 in RevPAR, first in occup and 6th in rate, thats how we run such high occupancy.

$115k per key, 11 cap going in, downtown El Paso.  25 yr amo, 1.54x DSCR.  Fee.  New asset basically, opened Jan 2016 after a $141k/key renovation and reflagging form Holiday Inn.  120 revpar penetration.  Military big demand generator. Post Bid Add: this was a preview they sent us and listened on feedback on no IO.  We need to check on the new supply still because not best building anymore.  Sponor BK mitigated by other guarantors on carveouts, hard lockbox in place and independent directors.  $140/sf for one of the few best buildings in Boise – were 2006 build and once past the near term roll which has $500k up front to address, the roll is pretty granular.  The submarket is only 10 buildings of comparable class A space but generally tight at 7% vac with rents of $23/sf gross but we did have two new buildings come to market adding 581k sf which was about a 30% increase in class A CBD space- worried that tenants want to move to the new best thing.  They don’t give us the two new buildings in our rent comps page so need to ask how well occupied those are and their rent levels.  Our top tenant is Regus and don’t know their sub-occupancy but they did just extend through 2022.

Late Add: four GLs secured by 1.5mm sf of industrial around the vegas airport.  11/sf - weird structure with the GL pmt not set at fixed amount, it’s a participation so in certain years the GL pmt dindt cover the debt serivce on our fee position.  The GL pmt participation is calc'd after the leasehold debt service.  the leaseholds are financed with fully amortizing loans that mature by our loan maturity and we have full discretion on adding new debt onto the leaseholds.  Guarantor has $11mm liquid.  from 2011-15 sponsors put in $2.2mm to cover shortfalls - the occupancy at improvements is getting better.  broker likes the operator of the leasehold.  CW not digging it - think should be a bank loan with recourse.  1.0 loan with $15MM on it that paid off.  if we can FC the leasehold debt out then OK, if not then TKO.

The kroger here was corp owned but they couldn’t expand so they left and built a new store.  in 2016 a Costco opened to the west of us.  good location but how do we know impact of losing Kroger to our sales / rents / occupancy?  can they build on the pads and it be our collateral? is that REMIC-able?  top tenant is a bar / grill restaurant that rolls in 2018, 24% - shouldnt drive much traffic from Kroger- have shown interest in renewing. CF and occup up from 15 to 16 and then to t12 so good facts.  the new lease is one of the lower rents but not bad at $18/sf.

Art Institutepaying $39/sf NNN for ground floor - that replceable?  were the cheap alternative to downtown but still dont love being on 17th.  probably best as a multi the colliers guy says but not sure iagree.  as Kevin points out, Art Institute has space across the street and is not a good credit.  they are the single tenant in the 78k sf building across street which has 12k sf available, sublease from them for 9k sf then the ground floor retail space is available.  no strats on enrollment

flea market in Hawaii - not in great condition and very very specialized.  $1MM mezz from a crowd funded platform, ugh.  $322k to refi from bwr.  Very grnaular and all roll all day. MLS UW to 1.09x NCF whch is right on top of 2014 NOI, MLS NOI in between 14-15.    ground lease requires capex to be over the hold, trying to reserve for it thru up front an ongoing.  $750k total.  pmts step up in 2019 and in march 2029 theres an increase that is TBD>  sponsor who's been managing for 20 yrs may be on way out.  feels like one of biggest issues of stuff in play.   one broker says she wouldnt want the listing to sell.  colliers tried to sell and couldnt get more than 12-14mm offers so they took it and refi'd at appraisal of $15mm

Sponsor: Bliss Properties, Lane Family Trust and Mark T. Brennan
ZF Comments:
Preview - needs lower proceeds + amo + guarantor.  The mall is appraised at a 6.25 cap and sized to a 12 debt yield.  10 yr IO, $12MM cash back.  Local ownership is three families who built the property in 1970.  In line occupancy not great at 87% but sales pretty solid, $478/sf for all tenants under 10k sf (comp in lines at $510/sf, 13% occ cost)and we’ve got Macys anecdotally doing $50MM ($277/sf), Target at $28.6MM ($204/sf), JCP at $20MM ($144/sf) and Jordan Furniture doing $27.7MM ($243/sf).  No Apple store, nearest one is in downtown Providence.  Theater does $467k/screen, 24% occ cost rolls in 2021.  There was a significant flood in 2010 and between insurance money, sponsor equity, and tenant investment a total of $125MM was put into the mall ($10mm from insurance, $10mm from Sponsor and $105MM from the individual tenants – Macys $34MM, JCP $10MM, Target 10MM etc.).

JCP rolls in 2020 – theyre a ground lease and only pay $110k per year plus % rent.  They do 24% above national average and have a 1% occ cost.  Store within a store here.
Jordans Furniture – here since 2011, chain only has 7 locations but they do $28MM almost for a 3.3% occ cost.  Owned by Berkshire Hathaway.  Roll in 2021.
Nordstrom was built from ground up in 2012, lease to 2022.  Don’t report.

The former Rhode Island Mall is almost adjacent to us and is being redeveloped.  Nearest true mall is Providence Place right in Providence, a GGP mall in DBUBS 2011-LC3 and is pretty kick ass.  Wouldn’t say this is really competing.  There is a power center a few miles from us with a Whole Foods, LA Fitness, LL Bean, Party City, Crate & Barrel and Sephora.  Some other mall comps are Swansea Mall (22 mi away, Sears Macys and WMT) and Emerald Square (25 miles away; JCP Macys and Sears)

I like the sales trends on in lines, T12 about 2% down from 2016 but above 2015 by a good clip.  NOI since 2013 also stable and on good trend up.  The occupancy costs have been pushed from 15 though, they were 10.7% now 13.2% despite sales up.  We’ve got pretty heavy and steady roll through the middle of our hold which is scary at a smaller market mall like this – JCP in 2020, then $3.8MM of income in 2021, $2MM In 2022 and $2.5MM in 2023 for a cumulative 73% through ’23.  Macys owns their box and is out of their operating agreement so can close at any time.  Target owns their own space too.  335k people in our trade area with avg HHI of $72k. 

Have questions on Sponsorship – we’re obviously at the phase where the original developers kids will be running it and want to understand how the children of three families divvy up responsibility and economics.  I do like that they put in $26MM for Nordstrom Rack in 2012.  Guarantor is Warwick Mall OP LLC – appears to be our Borrower which is BS. They say min NW/Liq of $17.5MM / $5MM.  

Tough call – not a no brainer by any stretch.  Would like to see a rent roll and individual tenant sales

ZF Comments: $90k/key for HIX with a year IO, built in 2003.  Toledo MSA.  1.77x DSCR.  Coming off IQ15.  had a $7k/key pip in 2017 to upgrade.  99 / 105 / 105 penetrations. Sweep a year before flag roll and full recourse if lost.  combined $35MM net worth, 1.7mm liq.  sponsor has other hotels in the market - Toledo - so can game us if things get bad.  good QA, recently did a $7k/key PIP in 2017.  what is penetration? discrepancy in write up - per ASR were at 99 / 105 / 105 so pg 1 narrative wrong.  covered all thr eway thru 2009, wouldve been 1.13x DSCR on 09 trough cf.  new supply.  visibility bad, were in behind a rough retail center which sponsor owns. worried abt PIP and gaming with sponsor controlling much of the market and building new supply, will be cashed out here.  we have done Formula Blue.  broker thought 110k/key today.  loss at flag, ex b and still dont like.  CB:  Loss towards the end.  market has been improving.  hotels tend to trade on room revenue multipllier, should be 2.5x for here.  flag here in 2024.  PIPd recently and didnt get extension; thinks we should be at $60k/key at maturity = $1.3MM loss at maturity.  age of this one and newer HIX is a problem, itll be big expense.  $15-20k/key PIP at flag roll.  debt equals 3.17x room revenue multiplier which is above market for valuation.

ZF Comments: BNK10 but no ASR on it.    $116k/key, over 2.00x DSCR and no IO.  2001 build.  128 rooms in northern NJ. Short Form: fractured condo in Largo, FL.  25 yr amo.  Going in at $80k/unit.  144 units of 175 are our collateral. BNK10.  landover MD not a good start.  Front door to leasing office looks really rough.  $22k/unit. Short Form: west KC apts, on KS side.  10IO at $38k/unit for 1971 bld mansard roof multi.  $7mm cash out on a $13mm loan, refi from JP13LC11.  10 cap, 2.78x DSCR.  Apparently per trepp in 2011 the NOI was $227k, then 2012 was $700k, then it was UW to $1MM and now up to $1.3mm.  sold in 2011 for $2.5MM and they put in $5mm post acq.  20% of units master leased to a community college on a 1 yr deal.  2016 murder, 2017 shooting.  not disclosed by MS was a tripl shooting in 2012 too.  2 stars on apt ratings, 2 star for safety subcat and defintely mentioned in the reviews; from the most recent one:  "cops are here every night for something" "lots of drug dealers always bothering us" "i've had to call police multiple times becaue it sounds like someone was in danger"  ....meanwhile, NOI been stable.  

per CoStar:  The property had been foreclosed on in November 2006, and was held on to by the foreclosing entity until now. For more information on the foreclosure, please refer to CoStar COMPS #2112236.

A source deemed reliable reported that there was significant deferred maintenance factored into the sale price. They will need to repair some major water damage to the property, as well as the electrical and hot water systems. Our source reported that a major portion of the buyers' $4 million loan on the property will be used for renovations.

Another source also reported that the property's occupancy was approximately 55% at the time of the sale. This vacancy, along with the aforementioned deferred maintenance, accounted for the building's very low price per unit figure.

$180 psf 10IO  on Tier III data center outside of Philly.  Built specifically for GSK for their own use adjacent to a big campus.  2009 build.  Sponsor bought in partial sale leaseback from GSK for $65mm.   GSK built for $110mm.  Bwr leased the entire prop on net basis for 13 yrs to Flexential, an operator who is running as retail colocation and GSK took 55% of the IT load for 10 yrs.  we have a 4,600 kW capacity, GSK takes 2,540 of it.  $12mm of the purchase price is for equipment so in the RE its $53mm and bwr has $55mm cost basis.  we have $18mm cash behind us.  bwr is the tenant, owned by GI partners PE fund.  they are the 12th largest data center provider in the world and have $485mm of revs.  we mature at a 12 debt yield based o the master lease pmt.  the GSK lease for their 55% would cover the Felexential master rent at 1.33x so they dont even need to be generating other income, though GSK sub lease expirse in 2026.  why did they spend $110mm to build then sell for a loss and lease back space? do they have other facilities>? seems like they put up a BS entity onm the carveouts.   note says that GSK built at time of rapid expansion and its flatteend since then so they werent using the whole thing.   seems like good facility and GSK has a need for something near their campus but dont fully understand the mkt for this.  Flexential just loaded up on debt and was recently downgraded.  they seem about 6x leveraged, Moodys says need to get below 5x to get upgraded at all.  5 yr loan would be better
ZF UW Review Comment: Philly area data center, $182 psf 10IO.  2017 acq by the sponsor as a partial sale leaseback, GSK built in 2009 for a massive cost basis and sold at a loss to our sponsors, kept half the space on a lease and the sponsor operates the other half thru their co-location operator Flexential.   GSK R&D facility is adjacent to the subject.   sponsor basis is $55mm for the RE and they paid an additional 12mm for equip.  MLS UW to $3mm NOI.  The Peak 10 / Flexential lease goes to 2030.  If we look just at the GSK lease were at about $2mm of NOI and cover out $1.6mm debt service.  Tier III data center with solid critical load capacity.  GSK rolls in 2027 and has other data centers, they can downsize in 2020.  we do have direct private fiber lines from the GSK R&D and HQ to this building, guess thats good?  tenant is B-.  broker sold a shell for $170 psf in VA, said turn key facilities like this would trade for 80-110 per MW which is like $200-250psf for this.   MLS put together supplemental ananlysis, think were worth $20MM on GSK only after a downsize and $32MM if they stay in full space but no credit to flexential... big big issue if GSK gone.   see josh maes feedback, bad location, not a great business for the tenant.

IF no tax assessment we should be fine to make it to maturity.  no sales bad visibilyt, think w need maturity loss.

ZF Comments: Tribeca retail at $780 psf, 8.3 cap we have Barrys here.  They renewed in 2021 with bumps for 5 yrs to 2026.  Gobrands signed for 7 years in july of 21, they pay $105 psf.  Been seeing them pop up, they raised $1bn at 15bn valuation- ocnvenience store style front and then warehouse style back of house to do deliveries.  this was a 2010 redev as a condo project, the retail is 100% leased and the resi is fully sold out.  gopuff is 56% and barrys 44%.  barrys here since 2012.  barrys got relief i/a/o 225k- why do they say its only 3 months when that is their total annual income?  the gopuff lease is well above barrys, barrys at $67 psf and there at $105.  we do not cover without either, pretty close without barrys.  we hgave 7 parking spaces too.  4 of 7 are monthly.  our units have 11.8% of the ocnod unit and are liable for common charges.  sweeps for both.  history not good, 2018-19 tis only did $300k NOI, then negative NOI for 2020 and then T12 of $207k and uw to $600k.  who was here before and is the gopuff rent sustainable?  seems like it was a maserati dealership before then was dark for a couple years.

Cinci warehouse at $75 psf.  1947 build wow.  Acq loan.  10IO.  10.4 cap 2.15x DSCR.  Single tenant thru 2040, FKC, lease is guar by Frisch Holding- a restaurant chain with 6k employees.  This is a commisary kitchen for the brand.  Here since 2012 and did a sale leaseback in 2019 with the seller, why are they trading to us now? tenant does $19mm operating income.  no outs.  we have processing, packagin, storage here- 30k sf dry storage, 20k sf mfg, 11k sf freezers and 9k sf cold storage and 7k sf cold docks.  industrial rents are $5.35 psf and were paying $8.50 psf so clearly downside here to the rent.  $4.65mm cash from sponsor to buy this.  infill good location but issue will be retenanting.  need to see tenant financials
Jan 2020 loan.  22.5 yr amo.  Loan went into sweep during COVID, trapped $9.3mm.  All hotels support rail industry.  No thanks.

ZF Comments: 2008 bld Hampton, $87k per room, 12 cap 1.75x DSCR.  5.55 coupon.  Small cash in refi. Middle of nowhere, 40 miles outside f CO Springs. full svc Sheraton in Metairie, right at a higway exit.  25 yr amo, 2002 bld.  15 cap 2.10x DSCR.  $1.4mm cash in to refi.  $1.3mm remaining PIP being held up front.  New flag for 20 yrs.  Sponsors only own a few hotels.  They bought this at auction in Jan 2021 for $15.5mm and is midway thru a $2.7mm pip.   like that it traded above our debt at autction.  adjacent to a big office xomplex that is 93% occup.  say its alot of copr demand.  about 105 revpar pen.  BSP UW us to 2019 which is will inside of the t12.  sept 2021 NOLA got hit by Ida which closed 5 compeitive hotels all coming back online.  we had big REMA room nights to explain our bounce back.  this is why they uw to 2019, and they show an adjustment basically normalizing the FEMA months but still guessing.  appreciate the work and approach but no way to know.   maybe as a basis play at $72k/key its a no brainer but dont think so.

generally inclined on colege town hotels but ugh.  In an old 1968 tower.  5.5 coupon.  11 cap.  Cash in refi $2.9mm.  The physical structure is four condo units- retail, parking, hotel and resi.  We are the highest share at 32.4%, the resi is slated for future redve at the end of this year and its currently in shell condition.  bwr bought prop out of FC in march 2014, had been in receivership since 2011 as a former holiday inn and office tower that was mostly vacant.  2021 had a good bounce back it did 1.66x DSCR, above 2019.  2020 was negative NOI.  no thanks.

Dialysis center in brooklyn way out by Brighton Beach.   10IO 1984 bld 2018 renov.  $940 psf WOW.  8.5 cap, 1.76x DSCR.  $7.3mm cash out.  Bought it in 2016 for 13mm and put in $12mm to renov and bringin equipment.  They pay $84 psf, theyre on a 20 yr lease from 2016 so roll 2036.  the tenant is an owner affiliate dialysis operator, lease is personally guaranteed.  if sponsor sells company to an IG credit the guar is removed.  NYAKC is tenant, started in 2002, they opened here in 2018 after the sponsor bought and renovated, they did 36k treatments annually pre-covid but thats down to 33k now, which is only at 55% capacity  they have 11 nephrologists and 50 full time employees.  they also get medicaid reimbursements.  is the equpment ours if we FC? theyre counting it in the cost basis so would hope so  but doubtful.   sponsor net worth reported at $50mm and liquidity of $735k, the liquidity is not great and im sure the value of his equity is all int he biz

Bronx BW+, no IO 2018 bld 60 keys.  16.5 cap 2.56x DSCR.  $137k a key, BW member agreement annually.   Les than a half mile from Yankee Stadium. COVID; in april the tenant asked for relief- they did not make may or june payments becasue tenant didnt make may and june rent payments, they made these payments boht on june 12, 2020 and did not miss anything else but this did put them into cash sweep due to lease default.  VV paid back rent in mid june, by aug 2020 the sweep was lifted.  they cut the mezz in Dec of 2021 and sold to a third party.  do we really want to test this? yuge severity if theyre gone.

two retail stores, HUGE propertires selling farm equip.  $100 psf.  We saw something in 2016 of their stores and it was $60 psf.  DST.  10IO.  Menomonee and cambridge MN.  New 20 yr leases. Property Name: Hilton Q Fort Lauderdale Beach Resort
ZF Comments:
DBGS:  they’ve really tightened up the structure around the condo.    $175/key.  .  They cut the loan amount and increased the amo, they added structure to address participation, sweep if below 74% and need to get back to 80% participation and 11 DY to get out of it.  Appraisal has thorough job on what is owned by sponsor and what is ‘biz income’ to owner from participation units.  Got better sense of renov.  Toured before and after model renovated room and they understand how itll be collected.   Hilton will run the renov, they’ve been collecting.  They have $5MM on hand and budget of $9MM, so DB will hold the delta.  The $5mm that’s been reserved – how are the unit owners paying for improvements to their unit? Theyre really not, Hilton has right to bill them but don’t want to so they’ve collected this and are holding it out of FF&E that is collected.  Is any of this part of the contentions in litigation? If you went back to first lawsuit they address shared costs so could see it in there but sponsors won that lawsuit, now being appealed.  Rollover schedule of the condos participating is pretty staggered, they originally started as 1 yr term and switched to 5 yr terms, when new condo owner comes in they sign up for 5.  The appraisal has a chart that shows participation going down but this is due to sponsor buying units.  

Units up for sale? Money set aside for them? Not setting aside any money but they do have the ROFR.  They’ve increased from 17 to 38 that they’ve bought back.  Plan is to keep buying them, and as they buy it becomes our collateral in fee.  How do we perfect this where we’re adding collateral to a REMIC?  Sponsor is paying cash for them.  Very few units are third party financed.  

If they lose the appeal what is structure? Claim is for $8.4MM – we have recourse for losses that arises out of litigation.  This would be an EOD which triggers cash sweep.  

Insurance – whos obligation is this? Condo owners or Borrower? Obligating them to get $100mm of windstorm that has a split if they choose not to rebuild where our borrower gets $70MM and they get $30MM.  General property liability works similarly.  Process to verify that unit owners are carrying sufficient insurance? They need to confirm this with Alston & Bird.  

Future PIPs – treated like any other PIP, there will be one in 2026 and they will have sweep up to 125% of the PIP.

RC using 40 of 60k sf.  this is kinda nuts not sure what sponsor is thinking. Raytehon was in thru 2014, they left the 6223 building which was immediately backfilled.  then left 6221 a year later, Tucson call center mkt still not great they say.  had some other large call center space that was given back by comcast.  they waited this out and got centene.  Centene is still building out just not using full sf, Revenue Cycle has 20k sf not using.  they originally only using 20 of their 60, now using 40 of 60 and have plans to expand into their space.

UW to $1.34mm vs $1.17mm historically.

ZF Comments: $8.7MM cash out re-fi loan secured by Shnuck's anchored center in Springfield, IL.  Debt equals $62/sf.  140,000 sf center.  Taking out a $8.66MM loan on KeyaBank's balance sheet.  1 year of IO then 30 year amm.  Going in at a 12% DY.  Sponsor acquired via Auction in October 2018--Key provided a short term bridge loan.  Property was in a Brixmor fund that was winding down so Bwr acquired at a discount.  Did Brixmor overlever the asset?  Purchased for $11.55MM and reports an $11.7MM basis--so 75% LTC.  Sponsor and Guarantor is Carnegie Properties (entity) who is reporting $100.5MM in assets, $14.5MM NW and $12.5MM liquidity.  Calling it 44% LTV portfolio at a total DSCR of 3.15x.  Anchor:  Shnuck's rolls in August 2021 and DSCR without them is 0.87x.  6 month sweep ahead of roll.  Not enough!  Shnuck's has an OCR of 3.4% -- and anecdotally has rising sales?  Does that mean the OCR is also anecdotal?  Tenant has been here since 1996.  The handful of in-line tenants that do report have declining sales:  Pizza Hut, Subway and Star Nails, Hair Cuttery.  USPS is the #2 Tenant and CVS is the #3 tenant here... Top 3 TT's all roll between now and 2023.  #4 Tenant is Pet Supplies PLus who got a $65/sf TI package for a 10 year lease at $13/sf.  Hefty spend.  Spin is that they backfilled DollGen who had been there since the 1980's and the LL had to re-demise the box, and add in plumbing for dog washing stations and live fish aquaraiums for Pet Supplies.  Not a terrible deal if Pet Supplies were credit, but I'm pretty sure they're not.  Pet is PE owned and a lot of the stores (most?) are franchisee owned.  Tenant Sales (p. 14):  Shnuck's did $19.7MM in 2016,  $19.3MM in 2017 and $18.9MM in the TTM 22018 period.  Why don't we have FY 2018 yet?  3.4% OCR seems real, but why are grocery sales declining?  Grocery competition:  Ruler Foods (Kroger's own Aldi), Aldi and WMT are all in the market and have been for at least 5 years, per ASR.  My guess is that Ruler and Aldi are chipping away at the Shnuck's here.  A Shop N Save in the market closed and was converted to Country Market (5 miles away).  1.63x/1.40x P&I DSCR if we cut Shnuck's down to 2% OCR.  This is a great fact.  Issues are that USPS and CVS are also risks.  CVS is in-line and could vacate for a freestanding box the way DollGen did.  1.34x/1.11x DSCR if we cut Shnuck's to 2% and vacate CVS.  Tough to break this given the huge coverage going in.  A little maturity loss might be appropriate, but I don't see term risk given the high coverage,  We am down to $7.23MM or $52/sf.  10% cap at maturity if we write down Shnuck's and vacate CVS ($727K of NOI).  Maybe no loss.

LOFT can kick 180 days past Nov 2014 if below $450 and doing $180.   $500k holdback for this.  They can kick thru May 2015.  6 other tenants with co-tenancy and four with kicks. Joseph A Bank doing $327/sf (slight decline). Talbors weak and declining.

Short Form: $52,564/Keys for 1996 build in Salem, VA with no IO; 78 keys, 1.82x covg.  Refi + PIP financing.  Flag with Choice to 2025.  ASR has no info.  Full Form Post Bid:  Got financials, 1.56x in 2012 and nothing more.  UW to 1.96x. Financing a buyout and PIP for 250k.  96 build; put in 1.1MM since 05, basis of $5MM.   Sponsor owns another Comofrt in the mkt thats newer than this.  UGH.  Full springing recourse for loss of flag - flag has termination options every five years and ends right before loan maturity.    105k liquid.  106 / 83 / 88 penetrations; ADR pen stable and occup pen increasing so RevPAR increasing. The sale comps avg out to our debt balance. Fails the 100/100 test.
ZF UW Review Comment: OUT- Not closing

ZF Comments: Seasoning; $333/sf for 2007 build, with no yrs IO; signle tenant Verizon Wireless rolling 2018.
ZF UW Review Comment: $333/sf for 2007 build / NAP renovated, in Smyrna, TN with no yrs IO; 1.59x covg and 100% occ by Verizon Wireless (4,500 / 01/2018 / 100.0%); stupid, single tenant rolling at us.  5 yr loan maturing in 2020 and they roll in 3 yrs.  Stupid. Freestanding.  Sweep 6 mo ahead of roll, could cut rent to $35/sf and cover at 1.25x.  Per site visit store manager says access bad and prop underparking. Good traffic counts at 110k cars per day. Loss at roll.

Property Name: Aspen Heights - Texas A&M University Corpus Christi
Sponsor: Breckenridge Development 2014, LLC and BRG Partners, LP
ZF Comments: $58,000 / Beds, with 5 yrs IO; talked to walt on this when we saw previously from rait; "I am doubtful that this property would sell for a 6% cap rate given the size of the school and distance from campus.  I think best case is in the 6.5% range.  I have looked at this market in the past and it is difficult to find a site to develop closer than 1.5 miles given the school is located on an island.  That said, I would guess that there are sites available when you get to 3 miles from campus.  I don’t like how your loan will only be on one phase.  In my experience it significantly impacts the marketability of an asset when you have CMBS loans on different phases with different loan terms.  It would be interesting to find out if all management operations will be run out of one phase.  I don’t know enough about the growth of the school vs potential for new supply to give a strong opinion if $60K per bed will be a safe basis in 10 years but I would be concerned."
ZF UW Review Comment: up fronted - 5 yrs IO.  They asked us for mezz structures and kicked around idea of taking the mezz amo to pay us down but don’t think its working.  $34.5MM cost basis.

Sponsor: Lee S. Goldmeier and Daniel J. Cohen
ZF Comments: Short form for bid: $113/sf, 4 yrs IO, 84% occup.  1.72x covg, 50% tenant rolls 2018 called BioClinica.  2nd largest tenant is a gym on a new 15 yr lease to 2030? Philly MSA.  Submkt 13% vac with $22-23/sf rents. New supply - 200k sf just delivered and 150k under construc - has this absorbed? NOI down from 2012 and 13; someone mustve left.  $1.6MM base rent rolling in 2018 on BioClinica and WinPak.  Need BioClinica renewal to work. CoStar has 30k sf available asking $19.50 + elec and also shows financed at $25.5MM in 2001 but RCA shows trade in 2000 at $14.4MM and CoStar shows it at $12.5MM.  Post Bid full form:  saying that the 2000 purchase was at $12.45MM and was part of a big port, thats whyt he 25.5MM of debt.  Theyve put in 4.8MM since 2000.  allowing free release of 6.5 acres for new development.  RMF spoke to BioClinica and theyve put 1mm into their space and like it, and their executives live very close to the subject.  They'd been negotiated with the optimal fitness tenant since 2013 but ahd lease moratorium; $30/sf in TI for them and they relocated here from a couple miles west (had been at that loc for 5 yrs and LL wanted to double rent).  Claim 2,200 members paying $45/mo so thats about $1.2MM in annual revenues excluding any classes / apparel / food sales.  Spoke to Winpak who is happy with space too.  Comp setoccup ranges from 58 to 100% with rents of $20-27/sf but were asking 19 for our vacancy. Sweeping 1 yr ahead of BioClin roll will get $10/sf of their space bc of the IO period then another 10/sf thru ongoings by then so $20/sf total if they leave. They call TI 18/sf in appraisal.   per RCA, LNR just fc on a similar prop 3.5 miles away in same submarket (lower bucks county) with debt of $120/sf Oxford Court Business Center.  Very similar prop and is appraised at $70/sf at 67% occup.
ZF UW Review Comment: Up fronted - theyre asking us to restructure but I don’t think we'll get there.  Issue is BioClinica 50% rolling at us- this is their HQ but their space is split between the 2 bldgs so brokers saying with glut of class A availability that they are ripe for a pick off.  Taking them out and releasing at $19 and back up to mkt occup of 80% puts this at 1.00x covg.

ZF Comments: 1.02x for 2012 and 13, then jumped to 1.32x, and uw at 1.58x. $700k for Dollar Tree - new lease with May 2015 start date on RR.  Near Dayton mall.  Minimal info in ASR.  Full Form post bid: sponsor issues, this loan at $4.8MM in maturity default with LNR.  They'll take him out in full. and fun Dollar Tree  reserves of almost $1mm.  Bwr bringin 500k to close.  DT backfilling chunk of space. Prestige is 100% occup, Alex 84%, Yankee is the weak one and will go from 44% occup to 73%.  Moving in July 2015.   based on UWNCF 171k of CFADS, no tenant individually breaks - Davids Bridal is top at $137k gross rents., Dollar Tree 2nd at $132k.  Dollar Tree is 10k sf and going to cost 700k to retrofit from Ballys.  Fam Chrisitan tenant at Prestige is in BK. Bojangles at Alex Plaza gave back half their space in November.  Sponsor DIL'd 2 props and has 2 in SS now, one of which is the subject loan.  Sponsor being sued by lender on LBUBS 07-C1 loan for misapplying rents. Sponsor denies and is settling apparently for 25k.   They got good broker feedback - wholefoods under construc down street from yankee which will help.  Early rollover, except dollar tree every of the top 3 at each prop before 2020.  Comp set similarly occupied so were competing  Weak covg.
ZF UW Review Comment: $68/sf, Dayton area.  Existing loan in CMBS deal in maturity default.  per MLS Prestige tenant fam christian in BK so potential closing.  Alex plaza immediate area every prop has at least 1 vacancy so competing for Ts.  Yankee poor condition.  Bally was dark for 7-8 yrs.  DCSR without christian bookstore of 1.24x, with no credit to Dollar Tree also then drops further to 0.97x (this is wtih both out).  Did not cover for t-12.  Midland notes weak occupancy across bwrs entire port, they own 16 props and 5 of the 11 retail props are below 80% occup.  Weak tenancy and poor curb appeal to the properties.
Dolar Tree future anch, 44% occup now, will go to 73% occup.  Balleys was dark for 7 years.
Anytime Fitness anchor, 83% occup.

ZF Comments: Suburban Charlotte office, built in phases and currently 81% leased only.  $1.1MM cash out.  No IO, $92/sf and 1.51x covg, granular rent roll with 111 tenants and no tenant over 9% of income - Regus leads at 9% rolling Feb 2017 and then AT&T in 9% too rolling 2018 paying a monster rent - $82/sf. $500k up front for TI/LC.  sponsor with numerous BKs - 10 loans with $170MM and some other FCs / DPOs.  Subject hit 54% occup in 2013 but theyve put $6MM into it since and have leased back to the current 81%.   40% of tenants are on shorter team deals, <= 3 yrs but 2/3s of these guys have renewed before.  35 leases (new + renew) with only 6 needing TI.   Parking not our collat but have perpetual easement.  Only 0.76x covg in 2013, up to 0.94x for 2014,  T12 up to 1.18x and UW at 1.51x.  Occup on a 13 year trend down since 2000 and only recently took its turn for the positive in 2013.  The Panera here does v well at $620/sf in sales.  Heavy 2017-2018 rollover with combined 50% of total income, and comprised of some of the higher rents at the property (incld AT&T).  New tower getting built on spec nearby but they say class A with rents in the $30s vs ours in the $20s.

10 yr IO at $40k per unit.  We have seen some before, we put losses for bid on GC32 Pangea11.  These are south side Chi multis, 16 of them.  Props surpassed 90% occup only in July 2015 so freshly stabilized, very late cycle.  No warm body.   They’ve put $7.3MM into these after buying for $3.3mm, cost basis of $10.6MM after buying from 2012-15.  They will have $3mm behind us.  2.42x covg and a 13% cap going in.    They did UW a 28% jump in taxes based on pangea yr 1 budget.  we dont have a full t12, no history, nothing.  All 12 mo leases, 43 units are sect 8 thru Chi Housin Authority program like Sharp Rose.  What happened with 6224 MLK - 0% occup for 4 months?

ZF Comments: Best Western in Seattle MSA - west of seattle on another peninsula, called Kitsap, shows nicely.  87 bld but renov in 2013.  Across Puget Sound along silverdale beach so we are waterfront.   121% revPAR penetration; bought unflagged in 2012 for $6.8MM and put in $560k so majorly cashed out.   25 yr amo so goes from $91k per key to $68k per key.   A big naval base is the major demand generator.  5.1% coupon.  $8MM cash back. 1.18x in 2013 then jumps to 1.68x in 2014 and 2.03x for T12 on the 25 yr amo, SMF UW to 1.61x.

$65/sf.  NE Indy, near Castleton Square Mall (SPG).  Two mirror image bldgs on the same plot.  Sponsors are buying them, 89% occup, from an owner whos been in the deal a while and who bought out their LP in 2010 for $4.4MM valuation. Seller put in 900k since 2010 and incr occup from 69 to 89%.  History on the default is that they had a bunch of subprime mtg tenants and one JV Parner didnt want to spend money to lease back up so seller bought them out.  $2.8MM equity at close from bwrs.  Anther distressed comp at $43/sf (LNR FC but then lost svcing), stabilized trade at $72/sf that they call inferior - its 900k sf and financed in JPc30 for $56/sf.   An REO deal less than a mile from the prop is 57% occup and appr at $50/sf.  1.22x in 2012 and trending up from there. Strong revenue gains. Granular RR but concentrated 18-19 roll and generally too much debt, should be about $50/sf going in not $65.

Farmington Hills office, nice looking bldg for 86 bld, 1.51x covg, 10.4% DY at $110/sf.  94% occup, straight refi from CS06C2 which went wire to wire with no DQ though did get watchlisted in '12 after a renewal with RouteOne with free rent hit our CFs.  Sponsor Douglas Etkin has owned since developing.  94k sf.  Ramco Gershenson 35% of income rolls 2019, a big REIT whose HQ is here and has renewed once.  sweep a year ahead.  RouteOne is the next tenant, they do auto financing and roll 2022 with 25% of rental income, they have a ternimaion option Dec 2019 with a year sweep struct, third tenant is Raymond James rolling 2018 iwth 22% of our income.  Sponsor did lose 11 assets thru the recession but never filed BK.   We have roll and its chunky but buying the stable history.  Doesnt comp out - within 3 miles in 2015 we have the following:  180k sf bldg just transferred SS, Refi of 35k sf bldg at $72/sf debt we took in C4, 276k sf bldg 1988 bldg Farbman bought for $64/sf, 85k sf bldg financed in MSC23 at $72/sf, a 230k sf bldg traded at $87/sf, a 94k sf bldg in Southfield traded at $32/sf/

$100/sf of debt, $3mm cash out for 250k sf of suburban office in 2 locs in MS, 98% leased with no IO.  The largest tenant is Miss Dept of Human Svcs who is on the hook until 2027 and is 63% of total income, then we have AT&T in 24% rolling 2017 and some other smaller guys.  sweep a year ahead of AT&T.  DHS bldg has been 100% occup by them for 20 years and has just done a 14 yr ext in 2013 and is 2 blocks from state capitol in Jackson.  DHS does have termination option after 2021 IF space becomes available in a state owned bldg.  they do medicaid, medicare, child support and welfare.  AT&T has been in their space since 2000.   1.02x on NCF if lose AT&T  (adds back in vacancy factor they took out) - they are a call center, porting of all numbers within AT&T system and they do iPad support.  they just renewed in June 14 and added another option - have they always been 3 yr exts? thas a little odd.  Do we have historical CFs?

Sponsor: Moiz Bhamani; Harwinder Kang Family Wealth Trust; Harwinder Kang; Moiz Bhamani Family Wealth Trust
ZF Comments: $55/sf for League City TX retail at 1.25x NCF covg, $1.15mm cash out.  Palais Royal, a dept store owned by stage, is top tenant who rolls 2019 doing $100/sf and 10% OCR.  We also have a Goodwill rolling 2021 and a Church rolling in Dec 2016.  Fam Rollar rolls 2017 but doing $127/sf and only 5% OCR so should stay.   5 yrs no IO.  Sweep a year ahead of Palais, and 6 mo ahead of the Church. Palais is owne dby Stage who is cloing stores and 13% of our appraised value is $1.1mm total and for 3.5 acres of vacant land.  Think needs loss at Palais roll.  Theyve been here since 2008.  Bay Area and Godowill came in 2011 - wonder who they backfilled.   Losing Palais puts well below 1.00x, Goodwill would put NCF below 1.00x but leave NOI a bit above.  Space 5 on the site plan is a super awkward layout and likely tough to lease, same with 07.  We underperform the submkt by a significant amount, were only 85% occup.
ZF UW Review Comment: Up front - we told GS we werent getting there, it’s a tired center with some DM but the façade actualy shows nicely.  Palais Royal here.  $55/sf, 82% occup, WFB UW to 1.30x vs 1.47x from GS.  5 yr loan.  Stage parent of Palais is closing stores and wthere are better Palais locations in the area.    $1.15mm cash out, claims to have $8mm basis after buying in 2014.  Palais at 10% occ cost rolls in 2019.  Also family dollar here doing $127/sf and 6.4% occ cost.  FD below nat avg.  We also have a 15k sf Church rolling in Dec 2016.  sweep if they dont renew 6 months ahead.    Brokers say were in the older part of town.  They dont think the vacant space is leaseable.  Also have a Goodwill.  Broker saysa retail is moving sotuh and were about a 10 cap on value.  The other broker from CBRE syas retail moving north - either way its away from us.  Keep out.

ZF Comments: 1-pgr:  16 properties in Chicago, most in NW burbs, 6 in south Chi, 3 of those look like they may be in the rougher areas, 3 that are closer to the water on S side may be a bit better.   The crime indices arent really good - 8 over 150 within a mile.  1 miles HHI around $50k. 1.40x DSCR, 10 yrs no IO on 25 yr amo and a 10 cap going in.  $42k per unit.  Sponsors bought them up in 2012 and renov over last 1-2 yrs.  Their basis is $9.6mm so will have about $600k behind us, ouch.    About midway thru Yr 4 we amo to the 2012 PPs.  At time of acq, 68 of of the units were vacant and 27 were non-payers or DQ.  Now only 12 vac+non-payer.  39% are subsized - this is about 24% Chicago Housing Authority thru voucher program (FKA Sec 8) and the others are subsizied thru charities, housing groups, some HUD.    sponsorship is a Citadel trader and a fmr public schoo lteacher.  have taxes been UW at higher amt?  any violent crimes at our properties? Alot of concern but basis maybe right given covg on 25 yr amo of move down to distressed PP by mid yr 4.
ZF UW Review Comment: 96% occup multis around chicago, $42k per unit, $2.6mm cash out refi.  25 yr amo.   Bought at various times over last few years and claim an almost $10mm basis (PP of $8mm).  WFB wrote to 1.23x covg, 1.42x from CG.  Amo to just over $30k per unit.  Good sponsorship that started buying these in Dec 2012 and has put in $2mm in renovs.   they bought with high component of non-payers (27) and high vac (68 total). 15% sec 8; NW of CBD.  Never covered for 13-14.

population declining YoY and 45% sec 8, bought for 35k/unit in 2013.  159 crime index.  Very weak 2014 - this was while he was getting guys out and renov so makes sense.

83% occup only, declining demos.  Bought in 2013 for $420k, 67% sect 8.  high alloc per unit debt.  159 crime index. SE of CBD along shoreline.  Similar trend in CFs, 2013 OK then way down for 14 then rebounded. This one not terrible.
100% occup, 59% sect 8.  bought empty and full gut renov, got running in 2015. Humbodlt Park NW of CBD.  145 crime index.
93% occup, 60% section 8 and weak demos, south side.
100% occup, bought in 2012 for 37k.   134 crime index.
85% occup, 23% section 8.  Low demographic area.  Bought in 2014 for $38k per unit.  NW of CBD.  Adjacent prop is boarded up, see site pics on pg 22.
100% occup, began leasing in 2015 and bught in 2014 uninhabitable.  Gut renov.  75% sec 8.  This is Logan Square which is a bit better than the rest.
91% occup, bought in 2013. SW of CBD. Almost no NOI for 2014 and barely above 0 for t12, UW to20k.
100% occup near United Center, gentrifying.  66% sect 8, bought in 2012 for $255k and a loan of $310k.  Better of the bunch
89% occup multi, bought in 2013 and basis of $255k.  55% sect 8.
100% occup, 20 units, half subizided and bought in 2013.  $600k into this.   Interiors are pretty awful.
100% occup, bought in 2013, $322k cost basis.  Weak demos.   West side
100% occup, bought in 2013 and basis of $360k. South side.  Bordered by empty lots
100% occup with 1 ground floor guy for 19%.  Good arterial in Little Village.  Only 1 section 8.  one of the pictures has a plastic covering over the window. Buyilding is fairly disgusting.

ZF Comments: 1.25x covg multi at an 8.6 DY going in, Kissimmee FL at $52k per unit, 1993 build and 06 renov, 98% occup.  NOI trending up YoY since '12 but only 1.05x on NOI for '12 so net of reserves wouldn’t have covered.  5 yr loan, no IO.  Bwr basis of $14MM.  Good unit mix.  1993 built and in 2005 sold by developer to MCSNAB for $26MM, Key gave $19mm loanwith $5.5mm of that held for capex for condo conversion.  75 sold and will not be our collat, sold in 06-07 for $158k per unit - none owned in bulk or by our bwr.We control HOA with 133 of 208 -thats on the low side = 65% by count.  13 inidivual unit trades (3rd party to 3rd party) avg $73k but removing the three top trades we are at avg of $65k. Any planj to buy omre back?  This mkt got hammered in recession with submkt vac of 16%. Too tight for a fracture condo - should be at a higher cap
ZF UW Review Comment: Kissimmee fractured condo; 1993 bld and 06 renov, 97% occup.  5 yr loan, $500k cash back.  $53k per unit. WFB UW down to 1.12x and used a 6.75 cap to get to almost 100% LTV.  WFB used the hjigh end of sale comp range, shouldve added 100bp to that.  This frac condo should be more at a 9.5 cap going in.  The conversion happened in 06 and is well maintained but still we only have 64% of the units.  75 units have private owners.  We handle mgmt of HOA and control the association.  non owned units trading for $73k per unit is a good fact.   13 and 14 had higher concessions, have been bruning off.  Too much debt so loss but dont think get killed here, may be worse CCRE stuff we try to trade for.  Brokers saying mkt still growing.

Sponsor: William B. Fideli and Timothy B. Wills
ZF Comments: Saw and up fronted Monarch 544 at Coastal in the CCRE12 deal that we lost, that was 1 mile down the road equidistant to school - 544 was north of campus and we're south.  That was $57k per bed, brand new and only 1.22x covg.  This is 2012 bld, 5 yrs IO.  396 beds at $47k per bed and $35/bed debt.  Fresh & Sophs reqd to live on campus and the school is adding 1,300 beds almost.   Appears to lease by the unit not per bed.  Our rent per bed at $557, below the other new build deals in the area.
ZF UW Review Comment: 2012 bld student housing 99% occup, almost 400 beds at $35k per bed, 1.27x as UW by WFB.  We're a 1/2 mi from campus which has just under 10k students.  2013 ACH replaced original manager and was 100% occup for 14-15 school year and is 99% occup now for 15/16.  Acq with $5mm in from student housing people.  School just delivered 600 new dorm rooms and has 670 U/C for 2016.  several vacant parcels that are developable.  still a small shortfall of off campus housing.  0.80x in 2013 when mgmt was changed.  1.26x for '14 afterwards but thats running 100% occup.   were low 3 of the 4 rent comps on rent/bed (including Monarch which was a kick of ours but at $57k per bed).  Still too tight, too low barrier to entry so should keep out, there may be worse GS stuff though to trade, the brokers are very positive on our location and $550/bed rent vs. $35k/bed debt is not bad.  We couldnt take without loss, $1mm / $2mm yr 3-4 because of the risk of new supply and tight metrics at 100% occup. Still pending Walt re: kick or no

ZF Comments: $50/sf, refi of two seperate but adjoined single tenant warehouse / distribution plants with $3MM cash back.  Gruma Corp, DBA Missions Foods/Gruma is 58% thru 2021 and BBB- and Flagship Foods, 42% to 2024.  $1mm renov in 2014.  GLA is 45% foot processing, 24% cold storage, 24% warehouse, 4% office and 3% mechanicals.  The 1700 Desert Surf bldg is a leasehold fee.  In Dec 2014, Tortilla transferred titel of the land to the country as part of a bond agreement in exch for a tax abatement for 20 yr term expiring 2034.  This was done as incentive for Flagship to reloc their HQ from LA to ALb.  No ground rent but Bwr just has to keep insurance and county will join in the mortgage.   Sponsor is an NM guy. In 2011 Albuq Tortilla sold biz opps to Mission Foods, their parent Gruma is BBB- with $3.4bn revenues but was delisted from NYSE as they go thru some corporate restrucuring, this happened in Sept 2015 and in Nov 2015 Fitch upgraded them to BBB- so unclear whats actually going on.  SPonsor bought in 2012.  The PILOT is 10% of tax liability and the Flagship Foods tenant is required to pay this.  Lender can preserve this PILOT.   Both tenants just negotiated new lease terms.    DIL? pulled recorded deed from CoStar and thats what it says. Sweep 9 mo ahead of Flagship roll, Gruma  sweep 4 mo prior.  Fresh Express and Heinz former factories both traded dark for $88 and $75/sf but reportedly both superior.  No ASR: Albuquerque Tortilla per CoStar moved into bldg in Oct 2013, subj is right next to an Express Scripts bldg.  Submkt vac is about 5%. Co was sold to Mission Foods in 2011 for only $8.8mm
ZF UW Review Comment: Albuquerque industrial / processing at $50/sf with 2 yrs IO.  $3mm cahs out.  Wells beat it up badley to 1.05x covg.  CCRE UW to 1.45x.  Wells marked down Gruma rent from $6.20 to $5/sf - the brokers didn’t give comp data to support the Gruma rent and the other deal was Flagship at $4.15/sf.  The Flagship space was empty for 3-4 yrs.  Gruma is BBB- and we have Flagship on 10 yr lease so they should be good.   2015 Flagship came to reloc their HQ here and got tax breaks to do so.  Its well loc in ABQ biggest industrial submkt.  The mkt is a bit stagnant WFB says and we have roll of both guys - sweep 9 mo ahead of Flagship and 4 mo ahead of Gruma.  Sponsor convicted of securities fraud in 1962.  Gruma rolls in 2021 and Flagship in 2024.  Flagship spent $9mm of their own money to build out space and has tax abatement that theyll want to protect.  Risk of build to suit for Gruma? developable land around per broker.  Need to get color on likelhiood of Gruma renewing - this is crushed if they leave.  Wells gave us color that they think Gruma is happy in the space, nowhere for them to go and they renewed without even testing the mkt.  The build to suit mkt isnt economically feasible and would take rents of $9-10/sf to justify.   Prudent to UW loss at Gruma roll but think they renew.

ZF Comments: 7 retail assets around Johnstown PA - the biggest is 132k sf strip center at 93% occup anchored by Ollies who just reupped for 10 yrs in 2013 thru 23 and has a Giant Eagle shadwo.  14k sf 100% occup center in Blairsville to Advance Auto and Goodwill, shadow by WMT Super.  Advnace doing $300/sf, 4%.  then the other 5 are single tenant DG's.  Refi of an 06 deal, MSHQ8, and annual debt serivce is coming down by 20%.  Zamias is sponsor - LNR loves him.  Min covg since 2009 of 1.27x.   8 new leases signed in 2015, one of which is sponsor affiliate.   100% roll during term.  DG is Baa3.  We have sales at 4 of the 6 and they do between 2.6 and 6% occ cost.  Sweep at Ollies notice period and also for Zamias sponsro lease.  The CF's have been declining since 2009 thru 2014, then a little jump for T12 and UW with NOI much higher, $135k.   EGI has been more stable in a range of $1.2 to 1.3mm but the expenses have been growing.    Sales down across the board from 13 to 14.
ZF UW Review Comment: Pledge of cash flow to Fifth Third; Short term / MTM tenancy – two not open for business at our site visit; 6,700 sf not usable – warehouse space at back of the center; Goodwill new anchor not moved in and hadn’t begun work on their space

ZF Comments: GC36 up front - never roundtabled, lucky late drop to avoid tough conversation with GS.

Tabani, $420/sf retail in Hampton VA - 3 yrs IO, 27.5 yr amo is odd.  98% occup.  SMF did the apts here, we saw these back in August.  Lifestyle built for $220MM and Tabani bought for $50MM out of REO.  Poag didnt like the center when he looked at it and thinks generally a slow decline.  we are 98% leased with minimal roll until yr 5when we have 45%.  top tenant is Bar Louie thru 2023 then Park Lane tavern thru 2020 and Abuelos mexican rest in 2021.  restaurant heavy mix.  bar louie had a kick but is above sales threshold at $2.7MM = $400/sf.  Park lane also has kick but above by alot, $581/sf.  Abuelos does $287/sf, down YoY.  Overall sales at our prop are $450/sf, tending up with occ cost of 11.4%.   RAIT did a mark down to mkt and that brought occ costs to 10%.  there is a lawsuit between JCP (non-collat anchor) against our sponsor - $500k in reserve for it.  tied to failure to reimbusre for assessments imposed on JCP land.  3 tenants above 20% actual occ cost, RAIT marked them down appropriately but if you lost them were at 0.90x. covg.

Sponsor: William B. Fideli and Timothy B. Wills
ZF Comments: GC36 kick

CW put the Citi rent roll in the file so we dont see what they did per tenant.  The site notes in the area description that we have more industrial around than true office, they say we are at the low end of office in AA due to the location.  2021 losses but small, depends where they size the loan but $70/sf is $22.9MM value

ZF Comments:
Late Add:  5 yr IO on 10 yr term; 1.37x UW DSCR at $233/sf in Murrieta, CA near Temecula.  We’re 100% leased with a Dicks, 24 Hour Fitness (no membership stats), and Walgreens but also a dark Office Max (empty since 2011) so 90% UW occup.  We have a shadow Sams Club.  Kroenke bought in 2013 for $39MM, $276/sf at 100% leased but still OMX dark.  Office Max continues to pay rent and are on the lease until March 2019.  Dicks sales aren’t bad and up 29% from 15 to 16 but occupancy cost is really high at 14.6% on $9.8MM, $164/sf.  Walgreens occupancy cost also on the high side at 7% after the multiplier applied but they go to 2030.  Dicks lease to 2022 – a 15 year lease from 2007 so not surprising its above market at $19/sf NNN – we do have room to cut them and assume OMX is not paying but we get tight really quickly.  24 Hour Fitness rolls 2022 also and don’t know membership stats.  We have one of the rent comps, Quad at Whittier, in GS4 at $160/sf with no loss (Burlington, Vallarta ($600/sf sales, 3% OCR), Ross, TJX, Michaels, Marshalls, RAD, Staples, Dollar Tree and Petco).  Recent rents at our in lines are pretty impressive and they’ve pushed NOI from 2014 at $3.1MM to $3.54MM on T12, UW to $2.9MM NOI.  

They should really UW with Dicks rent cut to a reasonable occupancy and cut proceeds back – if we knock them to a 8% occ cost it takes almost $650k of gross income out and 1.05x DSCR with them at $9/sf base rent (cover at 1.15x with Dicks at a 10 occ cost).  Would also be nice to have some structure around Office Max – maybe have Kroenke master lease the Office Max space beginning on the day they stop paying rent or at least be building reserves while they’re paying – instead we have all reserves waived unless a sweep period exists.  5 yr IO also tough on this given the buy at $39MM also 100% leased now just closer to the OMX expiration, though we have gotten some rent growth.

OMX in 2019.  Dicks sales $165/sf and nada on 24 Hr.  14 spaces available in Murrietta city that are over 15k sf.  plenty of boxes.  Like that we have Kroenke next to a Sams but too much debt.  OMX at $500k income that expires in Mar 2019 is total BS, theyve been trying to lease and cant.  waste of loss since a late add.    Loss - 2022; needs a healthy amount too, $3MM, $5MM.
