We rank last in ADR, 70% index - its because we have the airline K but only 110 occup pen so 78% revpar penetration.  Lower liquiditiy sponsor, under $1mm.  Flag to 2026.  Didnt cover in 2013.

$26k per unit, 1.42x covg going in on a 10 cap.  Shooting.  Kick.  Bwr owned since 99.  terrible unit mix with tiny units.  2014 after the killing there was a big hit to occup.  Got back to 94%.  At trough after the shoooting it was 77% occup.  1.10x in t12 at the trough.  Name change.  comm 2006-c7 with

$35k per bed, 1.47x covg.  3 yrs IO.  Refi of student housing a half mile from USF campus, big cash out of almost $10MM.  Bought 2006 for $8.4MM as a multi, put in $7.5MM to convert to fully furnished student housing in 2013 now refinancing - 2012 NCF was $590k vs post renov of $1.85MM NCF, cost basis of $16mm vs $20mm debt.  Full gut renov beginning in mid 2013.  They did half of it in 13, more in 14 and finished all in Augst 2015 and is 98% occup for the 15-16 school year.  All beds are 12 mo leases.  105 of 570 beds are leased to a bulk operator who subleaes to short term students.  They lease thru 2018 and originally took beds thru Aug 2015 on a 6 month deal.  USF is 42k students.
ZF UW Review Comment: $35k per bed, student housing in Tampa a half mile from USF, $9MM cash out.  Sponsor bought in 2006 for $8.4MM as mkt rate apt complex at 70% occup with DM.  $2mm in capex thru 2012 then from 13-15 another $5.5mm to convert to student, basis is $15.8mm vs $20mm of debt.  units delivered between Aug 14-15, 98% leased for 15-16.  72 original build.  12 mo leases with parental guar.  105 of 570 beds are leased to THS for foreign kids in Busch Gardens internships.  Sweep starts Jun 2018 for THS.  1,000 beds U/C in mkt but still largely underserved.  USF has 42k students.  Brokers say were in a bad location.  THS has renewed its lease here a couple times and goes thru July 2018.  they rent at $20/bed lower than others.  Barc UW to 1.51x using budgeted revs and marketd up expenses, MLS UW to 1.40x.  Sale comps dont prove out.  M&M broker is awful- say were low income, high crime and people move in on lease specials and move out.  very few students would want to live here, the other side of campus is more walkable.  the ASR uses comps that arent real comps.  2007 bld sold for less than $40k PER UNIT near us.    Shows nicely actually but choking on the broker feedback.  Needs loss.  $3MM / $5MM to bring base to $30k per bed.  The sale comps in this neighborhood are not good - found the one that M&M guy brokered, Asbury, 552 units for $40k/unit in Dec 14, others in area typically in the high 30s to 40s per UNTI.    USF Gardens is a good debt comp, in CCRE14 at $26k per bed, people like it though.  Univeristy Square also good in UBS3 at $48k per UNIT crossed with 4 other props.  Kick Bucket

ZF Comments: SRO deal in Downtown LA.  $68k per unit, a mile from staples center.  $4.7mm cash out, bought in 2010 and gut renov, have a $20.5mm cost basis (18mm if exclude 'soft costs').  $4mm PP, $9.4mm renov, $4mm in relocation costs and $700k in TI/LC for the ground floor.  97% occup.  30% short term leases, < 6 mo.  Claim land value of $500/sf which is 100% LTV.  Renovs were done Mar 2012 by Jan 2013 it was up from 0 to 52, then to 80 2 months later and 92% since.  There are 10 SRO's in the immediate area so its accepted product type.  sponsor is being sued for poor living conditions at a property of his and the suit is still open with 6 tenants left to settle (est at $150k but still).  2 of the rent comps are under 75% occup, 3 are in high 90s.  CFs very stable but dont like property type.  Retailers all roll.  Pulled land comps and checks out netween $325- $550/sf
ZF UW Review Comment: SRO - kick.  Already told them.  Could be fine but horrible precedent

ZF Comments: SW Indy by the airport, $35k per key and big covg.  Refi with a $1.2mm being held in reserve for future PIP and a $2.9mm cash back.  Sponsors have owned since 1967 and built it.  2011 put in $11mm to reflag from Holiday to a Crowne Plaza, that flag now expires in 2019.   It will need a 2019 10 yr PIP that is est to cost 2.4 to 3.25mm then.  We'll have the 1.2mm now, then structured with $12k per month contributions until flag is extended.  BS that they count the FF&E as cumulative.  We also have a yr sweep.  If take their numbers and take out the FF&E they count then were at 8,400/key for the 2019 PIP = 2.26mm which is below their estimate.  Full recourse if they dont complete a PIP of if lose flag.   RevPAR pen of 115.
ZF UW Review Comment: $35k per key; full service Crowne Plaza in Indy built in 1967 and reno in 2008.  1.39x in 2013, up to 2.51x for t12.  no IO.  5 story lobby with good amt of banquet space.  $1.2MM for the 2019 PIP up front reserve plus ongoings, also a $2.9MM cash out.  IHG flag expires in 2019.  we lease some land from airport authority to accomodate parking, recourse if we ever lose this.  A new full service holiday inn (also IHG) opened closer to airport in early 15.  this is stupid that we have a PIP in 2019 at flag roll and theyre guessing at what to reserve for it.  Extremely tight margins at 11% in 2013 going to 18% in t12.   The closing of an airport terminate in 2008 really hit this hard.  Broker says it shows well and our sponsors are good, thought at $35k per unit we'd be OK.  Thinks we will get hit by the new Holiday Inn.  There is also an old Days Inn that’s going to get converted to Hilton.  We last got PIPd in 2008 so could be easily $3-4MM in work.  The CBRE guy liked that we've pushed RevPAR despite some new supply coming in.  Is $35k per key low enough?  $1MM loss gets you below $30k per key in Yr 4.  Kick Bucket pending CB.

ZF Comments: Sold Nov 2009 for $5.4mm, bwr cashed out now after putting in 1.15mm.  1.53x covg, 2 yrs IO.  1955 build, Houston.  100% occup.  Big NOI growth over their 6 yrs of ownership from $440k to $800k now while keeping occup over 90% when pushign rents.  Near the Hobby airport.  Holding back 500k more for further renovs. syndicate bwr, 27 people behind our sponsor with 2-6%.  Sponsors own 16%.  $40k/unit too much for this.  Low demographic area.  Only sale comp close was half mile away, 55 bld also and was at $44k per unit.  Loss.  0.96x in 2013 adn 1.14x in 2014.
ZF UW Review Comment: 1955 bld multi in HOU, 2009 renov.  1.01x in 2013, 1.12 in 14 and 1.15 in 2015, Barclays UW to 1.53x and MLS to 1.39x.  $3.8MM cash out.  $40k per unit.   The adjacent winkler villa same vintage sold this year for $44k per unit.  Too much debt, rough collateral.  stupid.  kick bucket.  declining crime rates per MLS but still see a bunch.

Sponsor: Nail Abro; Akram Namou; Malik Abdulnoor
ZF Comments: Dearborn MI extended stay hotel, 88 bld at $48k per key, 2.28x covg 17.5 cap going in on 25 yr amo.  Built as a resi inn in 98 and converted to Hathorn in 2013, sponsor put in $1.6mm in 2012 for that conversaion, then put in $870k more in 2013-15, bought in 2012 for $2.4MM, under $19 per key with 5 other Resi Inns.  Basis is below $5mm vs. $6.1mm of debt, stupid.  The hawthorne (wyndham) flag goes to 2032.  84 / 110 / 93 penetrations, only 57% occup is terrible.  sponsors are high net worth guys, over $100mm net and $12mm liquid.  sponsor is building a 99 room Staybridge and 108 room HIX 5 miles from us.  Selling this to us.  2012 is 1.53x covg.
ZF UW Review Comment: $47k per key, 1988 build Hawthorn in Dearborn MI.  Flag to '32 Big CFs but really low occup and ADR hotel, only $40 revPAR in 2013, MLS UW to $43 revpar.  Sponsor bought distressed for $19k per key with 5 other Resi Inns and a Troy one.  Cost basis is under $5mm.  new Staybridge being built by sponsor and an HIX being built.  Sponsor is wealthy guy but this is bad lending.  Sponsor shows bringing 42% to bottom line which is what Barclays UW- at occup rates in the 50's % theres no way.  brokers have good things to say about sponsors.  broker says 50-60k value.  CB hated, Kick Bucket

ZF Comments: $240/sf, unanch retail with heavier restauarant concentration.  Sponsor built in 06-7 and has cost basis of almost $500/sf.  $440k cash back.  100% occup, wer ejust off the major intersection of the area.  Restauarnt is TioJuans who rolls in 2022 and here since 2012, they are a big chain and were the best of the chain in the philly area$2.8mm in sales, $404/sf and 8% OCR.  Also have Osaka japanes restraunt thru 2023, post office to 2023, a bank branch - Beneficial Mutual thru 2027.  Like the long term leases but fail rate with restaurant exposure is high.   Complex org chart, alot of chefs in the kitchen.  They had some issues in recession but never lost anything.  Submkt not setting world on fire with 9% vac and $15/sf rents. NOI dropped in half from 2011-12.  Needs loss, pick a date but saw NOI of $300k.
ZF UW Review Comment: $240/sf, 28 yr amo and small cash out.  Restaurant heavy lineup, 50% comes from two restaurants on longer term leases, Margatia place to 2022 and japanese place to 2023.  good trend up since 2013.  30 mi N of philly CBD.  100% leased to 8 guys and a bank branch with drive thru on an outparcel.  Post office takes 11k sf.  2011-12 occup was 77% because they had 2 suites dark, econ occup was 50% because of a non-paying restaurant.   MLS appropriately UW a 10% vac factor - needed given the restaurant concentration.  sponsor built in 2006-09, we have lots of office around which helps the restaurants.  $400/sf sales at the mexican restaurant, 8% occ cost.  dont have Osaka sales.  0.78x on Barc UW without Mexican and 1.17x without Osaka.  sub 1.00x without both on MLS.    brokers say the mkt is pretty tight but seems like our rents are above mkt.  Needs loss for the restaurant component and above market rents.  $750k at Oasak roll.  Tio Juans may succeed, they do good sales.  <our site inspector drinking on the job, pg 20 at bottom>

ZF Comments: $67/sf, 1.42x covg 3 months seasoned.  Naperville industrial.  Bought 2008 for $70/sf at 75% occup and has put in $1.7mm since for TI's for 4 tenants.  They will keep $2.2mm equity in behind us.  87% occup.  Our lead tenant is a state funded K-12 school for special needs kids up thru age 22.  theyve been here since 2008 and take half the bldg and 73% of our income.  they roll July 2018.  They are in discussions to buy the property and extend their lease.  too soon - finish this and finance it after this all happens.  sweep a year ahead of their roll and $100k up front for them.  37% tenant Primus rolls in 2020 and does stone work, they expanded in 2015. if this ever breaks the severity will be huge because of the rent paid by SEAL, $12/sf and their custom buildout so if its not a school then what is it? no one else will pay that rent for it vs $4-5/sf for the rest.
ZF UW Review Comment: $68/sf flex space to two tenants, Seal South is 50% and Primus natural stone is 37%, roll in 2018 and 2020 respectively.  Cash neutral.  Good trend up in CFs since 2013.  Seal is a school, there are 2 other locs and the students get state and fed funding to pay tuition.  lease is guar by three people. sweep a year ahead of their roll.   submkt flex vac is 11%.  we are well located with good access, sponsor bought in 2008 and put in almost $2mm.  seal put in $2.6mm of their own money to build out space and has a purchase option.  will county where we are is a good tax haven vs. cook.  by the seal roll with up front 100k ongoings and sweep well have $440k.  bwr in discussions alreawdy with seal about buying the property and assuming the loan.   current bwr is 99% owned by 83 individuals.  ugh.  the school pays $11.50/sf vs mkt at $4/sf - loan doesnt work without them paying a huge premium so if we lost them the severity would be very high.   this is $30-40 buck a foot stuff.  sigh.    MLS wasnt allowed into the SEAL space.    doesnt seem like either tenant is going anywhere, Primus is building out a showroom and the school wants to buy the bldg but way too much debt all driven by the schools rent.  needs loss; stupid to do so close to the school buying.  Kick Bucket

ZF Comments: Champaign student housing, $53k per bed, 1.25x covg and no IO.  Refi with 92 beds, 30 units with 5k sf retail in ground floor.  $1.2mm cash back refi.  2003 bld, 100 leased for 15-16.  leases by unit with joint and several guar.  Last year they replaced all appliances.  Bwr affiliate in the retail thru 2030 as event space.  1,800 beds U/C in Champaign.  1.19x in 2012 and trending up nicely. bad miz with no bathroom for each bedroom.
ZF UW Review Comment: $53k per bed, $165k per unit multi / student in Champaign (U of I).  2003 build and pretty steady CFs around 1.35x covg historically but Barc wrote down and MLS wrote down even further to 1.01x.  $1.2MM cash out.  The retail is leased to the sponsors daughter and Barc UW the master lease income of $90k vs the $48k the daughter is paying her father, our bwr.  brokers say it would be dark if not the sponsor.need to zero out.  they need to fix the design flaw to change the non shower tubs to have shower heads.  fair condition.  lots of new supply and alot of smaller projects too.  plenty of supply closer to campus than us.  they had to switch to 10 mo leases from 12 to keep it full this year.    Kick bucket.    Early MLS: problem with only having 1 shower per unit, owner daughetr operating in the retail with a master lease to sponsor at a big number.  Broker says no demand for this as a retail location.  2,300 beds since 2010 delivered with 1,300 more coming in 2016.  also bldg dorms on campus and a number of small 4-10 unit projects from the site.  our resi loc is good and were competitive but will have hard time pushing rent.

ZF Comments: $55k per unit, NOLA MSA.  Sponsors bought for $21MM in 2002.  5 yrs IO and refinacing 26.5mm in debt so $3.3MM in cash out.  This is related to 2 others (in this deal, $15 and #30)  80s build and 03 renov,   1.42x covg going in on the JPM UW which is at 11% vac vs 7% actual.  NOI actually troughed in 2012 (was down for 09-10, rebounded for 11 and dipped back in 2012) at $2.53MM = 1.33x covg on NOI, 1.26x after this loans reserve. Its been working and shows nicely but $55k feels heavy for this vintage - nothing trading close to the appraised value of $75k, feels more like 100% LTV based on the comps but CFs work.  Good steady growht in CFs since 2012.  Expenses seem light though, around $3,300/unit.
ZF UW Review Comment: 1982 build multi at $56k per door, 5 yr IO, 10 yr loan.  $3.3MM cash out.  Stable trend up in NOI from 2012-t12 from 1.33x to 1.45x, MLS uw to 1.34x.  Class B.  Small units, the 2brs are only 880 sf.  Some units upgraded.  Bought in 02 for $21.5MM and has managed since, was in a Wachovia 06 deal and had some stints on WL for DM but PTM with original balance of $28MM.  Trough NOI in 2012 of $2.33MM, actually stayed in tight range from $2.5-2.6mm NOI from 08 thru 2011 then dipped, now rebounding.  MLS likes how we compete vs the comps.  We're in a good part of a tough area and specific neighborhoods vary with some higher elements of crime.  suprising our broker doesnt know the sponsor since hes owned for 13 yrs.  Broker says liquidity tougher here and higher cap rate mkt bc of the area.   Our in place rents are fair.  The 2012 comp at $62k per door for 84 bld in Gretna 2 mi away.    Seems like a good property in a bad area; needs loss for the lower demographics and poor broker feedback. A 1973 bld property a mile away sold in May 2014 - Gates on Manhattan, for $50k per unit.  2 Fannie Refi's within a couple miles of also slightly older vintage stuff at appraisals of mid $50's per unit.  Marking to $50k per unit for this would be $3MM at IO end - $1.5MM maturity and $3.5MM at IO end as base and DS.

ZF Comments: Read but notes disappeared - student housing at school with under 10k kids, achieved big rents on leases but never operated.
ZF UW Review Comment: 504 bed property in Kingsville, $43k per bed, 2015 bld just opened.  Damus sponsor, ACH is managing.  $4.5mm cash back, 100% LTC.    School is TAMU Kingsville with 8,800 students and growing.  Good loc in the market across street but low barrier to entry mkt and small school.  Our rents of $620 per bed are good but well above the only comp in the mkt (2005 bld, Javeline Station, $535/bed).  1.28x UW by JPM and 1.10x from MLS.Keep out, dice roll on where this stabilizes and low barrier to entry.

ZF Comments: Marietta GA multi, NW of ATL.   1974 bld, $45k per unit, 7 yr loan.  Only 89% occup, $2.2mm put in since 2013 and still not fully occup.  Sponsors bought in 2001 for $19.5MM and have a $25MM basis unencumbered - finacned in a 2003 CS deal with similar NOI then as today but in 09 it defaulted and was splie with an A and B, they put $3mm behind an $11mm A.  In 2015 they paid off both A and B for $17MM plus 800k of deferred interest.  no loss.  Like that were near where the new braves stadium will be. 2009 NOI would give 0.84x, 2010 at 0.90x and 2011 at 0.99x and a good jump for 2013 post renov; slower to recover though.
ZF UW Review Comment: $45k per unit.  1974 vintage.  7 yr loan, $1mm up front for DM, drawn down to abt $860 per JPM.  MLS saw this before and said it was a kick due to DM.  This was in CMBS with $18.5MM of original debt and was split into an A/B with $3mm equity in behind the $11.3mm senior and $5mm B.  full payoff now, think the A has some accrued interest bc the balance was up to $12.9mm at some point and we're told the refi amount is 17.7mm.  Appraised at $11.5MM as of July 2013 (10 cap on 2012 NOI). 1.32x in 2014 and up for 2015.  2010 NOI was $985k, $1.1MM in 2012 then 2013 up to $1.36mm.  A lot of the comps have been renov'd.  2003 CS loan, defaulted in 2008.  FKA Signature Place Apartments.  Awful reviews.  mold.  our owner has owned since 2001.  12% vacant now.  Our rents are very low vs. market.    Place looks like a dump.  sponsor gave back a 92 unit prop in Clayton in 2012.   Loss.  $3MM to get to $35k per unit in yr 4, $5MM down.

ZF Comments: Carrollton GA, $103k per key, 77 key hotel with big cash out above 06-C25 loan.  2.00x covg almost, 13 cap going in.  Sponsor built in 2003 and cost basis is 83k per key.  Flag to 2024.  103 / 109 / 113 penetration levels.  40 mi SW of ATL.   Trough at 1.06x in 2009, climbing nicely from there.  springing reserve 2 yrs before flag of 32k per mo so will get $10k per key and recourse but only for losses assoc with losing the flag.  A new CY Marriott just opened in Aug and an HIX is getting built, the existing HIX will lose its flag and beocme Best Westerdn Inn.   Tiny mkt and new supply so a bit of a crapshoot but we;ve got big covg and property shows nicely.  Flag in 2024 - think higher likelihood they renew given 03 bld.  Paramount is a good operator.
ZF UW Review Comment: $103k per key for 2003 bld Hampton, no IO.  Flag roll in 2024.  good leaps from 13 thru 15, MLS beat up from 1.99x in t12 to 1.51x.  They UW in line with 2013 RevPAR.  $4,3mm cash out.  7/2022, two years ahead of the flag roll, 32k per mo reqd deposit for 10k per key for the PIP in 2024 - will not be enough.  recourse ise weak with only recourse for losses due to loss of flag and the Qualified Franchisor language is pretty wishy washy too - comparable size, keys, value and no downgrade - we've seen JPM get this where they name them per STR tiering.  a new CY just ramping and HIX is planned nearby.  Had perfect lobby done in 2013 (see what the Hampton Largo needs - 2005 vintage with the same lobby program done and needed 17k/key).   broker likes our operator.  HIlton likely to get someone to build them a new Hampton in 2024 - needs some loss, maybe kick bucket if room.

ZF Comments: $82k/key, 21 cap and 3.12x DSCR.  No IO.  Appr at $200k/key jeez.  $2.1MM back to them, they built it for $9.9MM so have some money left, about $1.4MM.  30 yr amo.  Sponsor built the HoJo in Queens too, that’s in a 2015 deal at debt of $205k/key.  3 miles from JFK.  the comp set operates at $142 revPAR and we are UW to $100. at a $75 revPAR were at an 11 cap and 1.59x DSCR.   opened in Dec 2017.  not ready.  who knows where this will operate.  wouldnt really call this a JFK hotel.  much niccer than expected.  LG not totally freaking.

Lyndhurst NJ office, 1.42 covg and an 10.6 cap going in, we have a $2MM earnout subject to DY tests at 9.6% for first and 10% for 2nd million so that will hold at 10% if they get the earnout money.  5 yr loan, 2.5 yr IO.  $107/sf.  75% occupied.  1984 build but shows in good shape.  Aeropostale and Barnes and Noble are our leads.  Moses Mizrahi.  5.4 coupon.  giving them back $7.5MM above debt.  SL Green seller financed the deal to sponsor when sold in 2017 for $31.5MM.  oh god.  290k sf.  Aero is 24% rolling 2028, did they come for rent releif during their bk?  Knowledge POint 360 is 20% rolling 2027 and this is their HQ, a healthcare related biz.  Barnes is 30k sf, 10% and rolls 2021.  BE occup only 64% vs 75% in place.  spin is that the trade from SLG was put under contract while Aero was in BK.  so all the value creation is based on improvement in aeropostale credit?  Simon, GGP and a third group, American Branding Group took them out of BK and claim co is doing better.  why wouldnt SL Green just hold on until they emerged? why did SL Green only get this to 75%?  this is a bad story.  Aero is $1.5MM of revenues and on P&I we have 865k of free cash flow, $1.3MM on the IO so couldnt even cover interest if they break agian.  roll in term is minimal so we either maturity loss or assume credit break and have term risk?  not much leasing done since close.  when did SLG buy?  per costar this was 100% empty from 2008-09, then by 2012 had leased up to 50%, did nothing until 2016 when it got to 75%.  lost a single tenant in 2007.  all the press and costar has the trade at $29.5MM not $31mm.  net effectives $10/sf.  LG maybe get paid with loss... ton of distressed comps in area and if we break were not clearing $100/sf.  with this roster we need term loss.  schedule the loan too.

North canton OH industrial with some nicely built out office, collateral kinda choppy see aerial pg 6.  $30/sf, no IO.  NOI up sharply from 2014, from $1.5MM to $2.5MM in 2017.    some pretty bad sounding sponsor litigation per phone call.  Jnick not a fan.  The Goodyear Building referenced in litigation isnt ours but basically they took out EB5 buildings on a couple assets (goodyear and hoover) that are pledged equity in the Borrowers so more like equity investments in the Bwr.  lender declared technical default? how?  were part of the former hoover hq complex.  this refi is going to pay back the eb5 moeny.  sponsor purchased the whole hoover complex in 2007 after they left.  theres retail and multi too but thats not our collat, we have all the office and industrial.  one sponsor affiliate tenant. enviro issues too so requiring $5mm in surance.  top tenant here is 24% thru 2023.  next guy is 19% thru 2020.  then a 13% guy to 2023.  no one else over 10%.  really low rents, $2-4/sf industrial.   really like the sales pitch - OH an aerospace leader dating back to the wright brothers.    sweep for top guy, capped at $1mm.  10% state of OH can terminate any time.  $250k up front for them then we have 7% affiliate guy.  only 84% including those two so could easily get down to 74% with a 7% affiliate.   Myers just over $1mm in income and only $550k of CFADS.  wouldnt cover without Commercial Honing #2 tenant either, theyre $670k income. UW with some loss.  LNR colliers broker says basis high at $30/sf and would be tough to sell.

25 yr amo, $70/sf.  Telecom hotel.  $1.5MM cash out.  93k sf high tech telecom office tower.  1927 build.  84% of income is telecom, 45% of the GLA.  They pay $36/sf and the rest of the office tenanc pays $5.75/sf avg.  Century Link, Intellifiber, Level 3, Mid Hudson Tech paying $61/sf.  all roll by 2023.  cash flow very choppy.  $416k in 2015 to $1mm in 2015 now $800.,  they say theres a timing issue where alot of 14 went into 15.    $1mm held at close to build out 6k sf of office into suites.  what?? $165/sf for buildout when they get single digit rents? physical occup is 66% but half the vacancy is functionally obsolete. nfw.

better than the Petco Bloomington that we kicked from CSAIL but similar story.  Were adjacent to a Dicks and Home Goods.  DSW, joann and Ashely at center too.  New store.  $180/sf, 1.30x DSCR and sub 9 cap.  No IO. 23k sf.  Ridiuclous they give us a sales projection.  claim trending above $6mm which would be a solid store for them.  sponsor bought in 2015 for $2mm and put in $2mm to build out.  basis is $4.4MM vs $4.2MM of debt.  former circuit city.  how long was it empty? Dicks is on a long term lease.  were right across from Eastland Mall - CBL.  theyre wroking to redevelop the JCP there but mall is tier 3 only does $300/sf.  they just lost Sears and Macys too.  they got an H&M and PLanet Fitness.   no shot this is $200/sf dark.  Circuit City was dark since 2009.  ouch.  lease to 2027.  Dicks bacfilled a KMART.   petsmart not a good credit and debt trading at major discount, theyre Caa1.  dont like.

Short Form:  $5.1MM, actually a nice looking property from front.  2.24x DSCR and a 17 cap.   25 yr amo.  1991 build.  84 keys.  85 ADR, 63 revpar.  $60k/key.  5.69 coupon…. Not big enough loan to make a dent with PA.  $4.5MM cash out.  In 2016 they rebranded from a comfrot to a quality.  flag with quality, a choice brand, goes to 2036.  were near the mall and the Stoogeum - a three stooges museum thats open one day a week.  jesus.  110 / 99 / 108 penetrations.  comps include Holiday Inn, Best Western, Roseway Inn Days Inn and Hampton Inn.  sponsor built it and claims his basis is $6mm but owned since 1991.  way outside philly.  LG hates.

Vernon Hills - far north Chi.  Were right by Hawthorn Mall and across from a Whole Foods - were retail and office. Our office is medical, mostly Northwesten Hospital.  Newly repositioned so no history. 10IO on this is really generous.  2.11x DSCR and sub 10 cap, 87% occup.  NW Hospital is Aa2 on a lease thru 2031 for 43% NRA, good anchor. Nestle thru 2032.  67% of gross rent.  Nestle moved their HQ from Glendale, CA to DC back in 2017 on a 15-year lease.  USBNK notes:  $185MM cash out re-fi loan to Monday Properties and Willett Advisors (Mike Bloomberg's Family office) secured by 35 story Class A office building in Arlington, VA.  Property is Nestle's USA HQ.  Monday Properties reportedely developed Subject in 2013 with their prior equity partner Goldman Sachs and reports a total cost basis of ~$430MM including carry costs.  Bloomberg's re-cap:  Loan proceeds will be used to repay existing floating rate balance sheet debt held by Citi, JPM, and National Bank of Kuwait, fund upfront reserves, cover closing costs, and return a small portion of equity to the sponsorship who recently recapitalized the deal with over $79.5MM of fresh cash in December 2023 in conjunction with the buy out of their former equity and mezzanine partners.  UPB equals $345/SF.  Property is currently 86% leased to 10 tenants (including the two pending leases out for signature to Core Architects and Graham Holdings Company on a combined ~33,500 SF that is expected to be executed prior to loan closing).  Sponsorship has another LOI in process for GEF Capital (6K SF) that is expected to be converted into a signed lease for a term of 7-10 years. If signed, occupancy will increase to ~88%. $27MM cash out refi loan secured by 129 unit MF asset in St. Albans and Springfield Gardens (Queens, NY).  Sponsor acquired in 2016 Sponsor is Joshua Gotlib. Abatement: Subject is exempt from RE taxes for 40 years starting in 2016 under Article XI.    Apartments Housing Development Fund Company (HDFC).  Shelter Tax:  Owner shall make real estate tax payments equal to the amount of 12% of the total rents received at the Property (inclusive of any federal subsidy, less utility costs) (the “Shelter Rent Tax”). The Shelter Rent Tax shall not exceed the amount of real estate taxes that would be due in the absence of any form of exemption or real estate tax abatement. The Shelter Rent Tax is included in the final underwritten expenses.  Income restrictions:  Agreement requires that vacant units shall be leased to tenants earning no more than 50% of AMI.  Very low income... HPD will enter into subordination agreement with Lender.  Given HDFC technically has the fee ownership of the asset given the abatement + program; our Borrower/Sponsor owns the beneficial and equitable interest in the asset.  Approved rent schedule:  Studios:  $2,055 / month; 1BR: $2,468/month; 2BR: $2,753 / month.  Revised ASR:  UPB is $26.2MM or $203K / door.  Shelter Tax:

BANA 4/10: Basin bought for 58mm in 2022 at 79% leased and got to 94%  ... think submkt vac driven by state; address 2750 gateway - we arent chunky and they think that was inferior sponsor.  

Michael Stassi - this is the one true office park here in SAC.  people like being by the airport.  subj is older but well maintained.  were a bit tired, 2295 is the worst of ours, 2250 is the best of them.   some other submkts held up better but covid hit us hard because we have bigger corporate demand.  2,500-5,000 sf avg user.  25% vacant direct, 27% with sublease.  there is alot of availability with small spaces.  bunhc of stuff going to be trading around 80 psf.  paint and carpet and refresh is $15 psf and then an LC on top of that.  a full remodel is $60-70 psf so guys are just going to their tenants saying stay in your 6k sf but just pay me on 4k.  1 mo free rent for a year of term.  free rent is tough, LLs are 14 months out from collecting income.  some buildings are much nicer than these that have alot of small space to lease.    all of the  empty building comps are 55 psf to low 80s psf.  not all of it directly competitive because chunkier sf.  $2 psf effective rents to start.    how would buyer view? 93% occup is really good for this, but would be a 10+ cap all day... the roll and the WALT would freak everyone out.  5% commission on every renewal.    doesnt know a bank that would touch this.  absolute pencils down for lenders out here.  this isnt a govt mkt.  buyers would want 15 cap.  doesnt know a person that would show up to bid on this.    harvard park where basin street owns out other thing is bad.  basin has some decent stuff but then their point west stuff is getting clobbered.   $120 psf value, maybe $130 but the sheer size of the deal would kill people no one would buy something for 40mm here.  in next 18 months were going to lose 10-15% of occupancy.  lease comp at Creekside really ncie project down the way, 11k sf 1.85 psf rent 7 yrs 6 months free $15 psf TI and $10-12 psf LC (full 5%).  

Stassi on Harvard Park - hes u/c 308k sf HP campus in roseville sutter health took it then left and owner gave keys back, on mkt for a year asking 23mm, u/c at 16mm offer walked at 14mm and 9 months ago and now in contract for at at $13.5MM is $44 psf way nicer product way better area.  harvard park is a cesspool rumor was selling to state of ca to do a homeless rehab social development.  harvard park is reallyl problematic.    CHP is out looking for an HQ relocation and need like 2,000 parking spaces in 275k sf.  they wouldnt even go out there to harvard park but doesnt think theyd buy off on it.  a dept of water resources requirement out for 300k sf looking at HP campus.  CHP going to want somthing nice.

Rialto sale.  another project that traded gateway 2020 and 2555 natomas, delivered in 09and in 2020  but owned unlevered and decided to sell, this sold Q2 2024.  traded for 110 psf.  very high double digit cap rate because it was leased but had some known vacates 410k sf empty basically in brand new build stuff (not comparable).  why are we above mkt? bldgs pretty good and basin can put money into their bldgs.    some HVAC / roofwork needed so that is part of the 5mm in.  25-50 psf in TI.  $5-10 psf on renewal and 1 mo per year on concessions.  lots of free rent.  if we took to sell today? not much trading in the stabilized space.  american river drive up hwy 50 120k sf 94% leased for over $300 psf in q4 2024, multi tenanted late 70s early 80s bld but some sa a 1031 who owns the adjacent project and will be an owner user so thats not really a comp.  Wells Fargo Bldg downtown 90% leased sold over 10 cap.  Tri County Bank did the loan with Bank of Stockton at 40% leverage.  to get local and regional low levg with recourse, partial or full.  ethan conrad bought a few bldgs in downtown renaissance tower 30% leased 60 psf, arena gateway 30% leased 110 psf.  770 L Street at 75% occupancy.  9 cap rate range 130 psf stablilzied with state leases.  5-10mm trades in the 100 to 200k sf.  over 300k sf is very thin to sell here.  best to go onesies on these buildings tough to get them all done together.  if in the mkt today it would be double digit cap rate range on mark to market rents.   Crowne Plaza sold around 60 psf 2750 gateway oaks (ten-x).  not going to help.  could face rent compression.  Harvard Park - bought in 2016 for $130 psf.  worse than gateway and point west for them.  

Randy Getz CBRE:  going to send their office stats, Basin Street is largest private owner of office in the market.  they started in petaluma and did well there and also in Reno.  they are very hands on and tenant centric and will make deals.  maintain their buildings well.   theyre liked by the brokerage community.  tenants want to make sure they are leasing in bldgs with stable well capitalized ownership.  alot of buildings in south natomas that are smiliar to this.  plenty of competition.  suburban mkts are doing better than CBD.  there have been some sales that have taken place at $75-80 psf distressed by value add people.  37% availability rate, 22% vac and negative absorption.  TI can easily be $75-100 psf on new leases, double what it used to be.

Owned since 2002.  have put some money into R&M total of 2.4mm over last 5 yrs.  61 tenants, largest is an energy company. dunno basis and debt yield are compelling but very much class B old bldg who would ever buy this how do we get out.  mkt too bad.

tough areas, crime an issue on this side of hartford and specifically at the subject assets.  Almost all 1s and only 30% renovated.  Allocations a question - theyre releasing one of the assets that were purchased last year so cash neutral but giving him a prop for free.  costar says diff allocations so JPM not even doing it right.  these are fully cahsed out after less than a year.  comp from this month half mile from property 1 is 530 Wethersfield, close to half 2s and asset fully renovated including all the windows.

single tenant with bunch of call center space rolling in 2031.  no space for sublease goodfact.  Seller bought in 2019 for $22mm now selling for 21mm… tenant is sub of charter communications.  Peer set had a ton of availability, 65% avail and only 16% vac so ton of space coming back.  2 miles away  arrowpoint one sold in dec 2024 for $135 psf to first citizens bank so maybe a user sale?8845 red oak is empty 50k sf 2.5 miles away, marketing for sale asking $280 psf by CBRE with the existing tenant in place until Dec 2026 so covered dark trade.  what is this buyer seeing with the roll?

multi in a converted office building.  CBD $160k/unit with another almost $15mm pref behind us.  Taking out $51mm of debt.  The retail is only 48% leased in ground with a gym and convenience store.  National register prop.  Avg unit size 1,222 sf.  There is a hotel thats part of this that was sold off in 2024 for $34.5mm so theres another condo owner there.  EB5 debt converting to pref sheesh so its not really new pref, 3% pay rate subj to available cash plus a 4% accrual.  basis is 78mm.  85% of units leased to bulk corporate tenants, mostly on 12 month leases with smiliar terms to direct.  HS Prop Holdings here since march 23 if 52% of the corporate units, Gaetro Group here since 2019 16% of the corp units, and Stringbnb here since 2021 taking 6% of the corp units.  right near the AT&T HQ and also the KBH convention center.  sponsor is Alterra who has some high profile deals.  reviews terrible as an airbnb.  homelessness issue.  does the pref have any features of eb5 that we need to be aware of?   oer CoStar downtown mkt is 19% vac for apts across 17 buildings.  

Mart Martindale:   knew the bldg for ages and thinks they did a good job physcially on the asset.  Location cant change though, just a bad part of town.  Next to city hall.  The homeless shelters are on several sides of it.  Within a couple blocks.  three or four soup kitchens.   the SE quadrant is a fairfield.  not a walkable location.  running multis with airbnb and short term housing not good, youve got cleaning staff in and out you've got people that dont have pride of ownership etc.  value 100% LTV give or take.  can they fill 220 units if the corp airbnb users fail.  this is a lesser desirable area and higher vacancy.  statler residences newer redev 3 blocks north 220 units is 21% vac, lone star gas lofts 2 blocks N 300 s st paul is 13% vac.  alot of units have been delivered in Dallas and opex have gone up. 

Chris Gomes M&M broker on the hotel: tough sales process, good buyers local group that does all their own development.  there was some interest at higher prices pre-rate hike cycle.  not a cap rate sale was totally price pre key and pro forma - by the time they sold it for 33-34mm was a good buy in his opinion.  his guys showed no interest in the apartments.  they diligenced the fractional ownerhsip, no shared amenities.  think things improve when convention center opens and then some games espn has 3 months at the subject.  his buyer of the hotel wasnt really concerned about the homelessness.  more an issue for the hotel actually because the entrance is on the worse side.  even in dallas people are getting their arms around the whole problem and tolerance level is increasing.  guests who travel regularly are getting used to it.  the soup kitchens arent far from here.  alterra was open to selling it but no one came in looking to buy the whole thing.  tough sale now, not really a hotel guy but unsure how multifamily guys would look at it.  no clue why they wouldnt terminate the airbnb operators.  nobody wants to get into that business.  the airbnb world today is very fluid.  hes worked with clients who have used eb-5 as equityand some have lost their shirts but dont care because they got their green cards.  here the eb5 was debt and thats why they had to sell the hotel.  when they did this in the beginning they wanted to do all apartments and the city forced them to do some hotel.  the eb5 debt was coming due and thats why they had to sell the hotel.  dealing with alterrra was difficult they have guys in turkey, singapore, california, some local.  aimbridge was managing the hotel vs us with 15 diff managers.  when they were selling the hotel their plan was to kick out sonder and kick out the other operators and start renting as traditional multis.  units are huge here.  the floor plates are so massive so they had to create double corridors off the elevator.  rooms at the hotel are much much bigger at least 15x40, some rooms bigger some smaller.   Alterra was a referral for him from a guy that is at M&M who knew him from Ohio.  dealt with Jose at Alterra, his partner mostly overseas.  reputationally think fine.  they do some adaptive reuse and use tax credits etc.

DPO of construction debt and wip eof existing EB5 mezz… recapping with pref from a newer firm seems like family office of banco safra guy.  Seems like theyre releasing the empty condo for free because the 190mm dpo is on both parts and this loan is payingi the fulll 190mm, and the pref is maybe crossed because if were 98% occup with long term leases then why do we need a faciity for the TI / LC and carry costs to the tune of $35mm?   far more questions than answers.... how many sf is the other condo, its empty? looks like roughly 200k sf... the pref guys have only done 6 deals since inception? best i can find on them is its the son of the founder of safra bank and some guy who was at QIA.  the DPO is presumably on the full construction loan which is secured by both condos but theyre releasing the empty one for free? the EB5 mezz is a total wipeout?  govt termination options ugh, T1 can get out 5 yrs after maturity so refi very tough and will be competing against the other empty condo that the sponsor also owns.  the pref is $83mm made up of $48mm initial, $30mm for future TI/LC and $5mm for operating defecits - if our condo is stabilized why do they need 5mm for operating defecits unless they're crossing the pref with the empty condo?
